title: Poisonous Mushroom

propositions.label: The man's relief was not caused by the mere existence of poisonous mushrooms
From the absence of edible mushrooms, the man inferred something about human activity in the area
The man inferred that the edible mushrooms were gone because someone else had picked them
The man was stranded and in need of outside help

statement: A man saw that there were only poisonous mushrooms around and felt relieved. Why?
truth: The man had gotten lost in the mountains while foraging for mushrooms. The fact that only poisonous mushrooms remained meant that someone had already picked the edible ones—meaning people had been in the area. Realizing that a settlement or other foragers were nearby, he felt relieved that he had a chance of being rescued.
